messages.text: I have never felt any genuine connection with protagonists who claim they love their ordinary daily lives.
Despite being exactly that kind of person myself, a high school boy so painfully average that the word despair feels almost accurate.
I cannot imagine feeling satisfied with an identity this bland. A background character. That is what I am.
My name is Keita Amano. Sixteen. Second year. Blood type A, Cancer. Small and thin. I live with my parents and a younger brother. Nothing remarkable to describe.
No special talents. No hobbies I pursue with real passion. I did baseball in elementary and middle school, but only as a casual social activity.
My grades are decent enough, but I slacked off playing too many games and ended up at a local public high school with a low academic ranking. Otofuki High.
All my childhood friends went to different schools. My social connections reset to zero. Even now in second year, I spend every break alone with my phone or handheld console.
Some people call it being a loner. The occasional laugh comes my way, but there is no serious bullying. I am too forgettable even for that.
A girlfriend? Pure fantasy. I am a background extra in everyone else's story. Nobody even bothers to acknowledge my existence half the time.
If I have one defining trait, it is that my name sounds almost identical to a certain famous video game protagonist. Oh, and I love games. Deeply, genuinely, unconditionally.
When I play a good game, I am truly happy. That window between coming home and dinner is pure, supreme bliss. The kind of leisure that makes everything else bearable.
Still, there are countless people who love games just as much. In the end, even this is barely a real trait. Barely a real identity.
And so, my story, the story of someone like me, will begin in the most cliché way possible.
An ordinary boy who claims to appreciate his ordinary life. Approached by a beautiful girl. A setup so formulaic I can barely acknowledge it without cringing.
Except this is not that kind of story. This is a story about games.
